item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
The first image could not be sourced reliably, the closest being here. As such, it is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0 and has since been removed.

The second image is public domain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, right at the end it's specified that the restaurant had been closed and presumably remains closed to this day (especially with thou foundation's efforts now). Could have been a bit more clear with the initial description imo.

item.title: Wikiwalk Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
edit: edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show
- Hide

To link to another article about an anomalous fast food restaurant, I proposed the following edit:

SCP-1904 is a network of recreational plastic tubing installed at a ██████████ restaurant in ████████, MD.

to

SCP-1904 is a network of recreational plastic tubing installed at a ██████████ restaurant in ████████, MD.1

Footnotes
1. Due to their similarity as anomalous fast food restaurants, a connection to SCP-2915 is under investigation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Any reason they can't just send robots in? Yeah, it turns humans into zika babies but I don't see why robots wouldn't work. They took it apart, sure, but whats to say there isn't some other anomaly that only manifests inside when the structure is complete? It seems very unlike the Foundation to just give up on looking into this thing because it deformed some D-class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Absolutely
Completely
Horrible

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The sheer what-the-fuck-ery of this is so magical. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jesus Christ. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I always thought of the second picture as a set of screwed up Chinese sculptures rather than actual mutated babies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
eeeeeeeeeerrrrrrrrrrrrrrrrrrgggggggggghhhhhhhhh.

And by that I mean +1 for the "oh god what the hell" factor without having enough to detract from the piece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow this is a lot creepier than I remember. Almost wanna upvote again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Neat. *snaps picture*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: WHAT THE FUCK

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Anencephaly, I think. Neither would have survived birth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I once heard someone describe Edgewood as a Proving Grounds bomb testing dummy town that people had unwittingly moved into, and didn't care enough to leave

that could make for a decent article, come to think of it…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I grew up in nearby Bel Air and remembered everyone constantly characterizing Edgewood as the shittiest part of Harford County; I actually kind of miss its soul-crushing, decrepit atmosphere. Everything is too new where I am now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Having ordered lunch once- just once- from this very location, I'd buy it.

The premise, i mean. Not the babyburgers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have an old, parentally-ingrained mistrust of places like this to begin with— this was just damn creepy. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1295?

That element doesn't bother me though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One example where I think I have seen it only recently with the four "old" horsemen. I can't remember the SCP number, though. Sorry I can't provide more of them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wondered if it had been done; I hadn't seen any of those myself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jesus christ, that pic. i don't like the forced spread of birth defects (Incident Report 1904-B), I keep seeing such a thing more and more (first with the four riders, then with this… nah), but otherwise, it is pretty scary, as a great SCP, at least for me, should be!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Oh, look it's some sort of playgro-ohgodnopenopenopenope upvote."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: JEBUS CHRIST THAT SECOND PIC AAAAAGH.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, this is creepy! I love the implication that it somehow 'sponges' up deformities in the area, or even what was said above.

My only question is where the -1's come from. It's never really said. Do they emerge from any particular point? From deep inside the structure? From just any opening at random? It seems an oversight.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: http://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/fetii#Usage_notes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At least in (spoiler) Edgewood, Maryland. There could very well be others out there, however; the real reason why some seemingly random locations never switch over to the now-standard 24-hour operation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that's the interpretation I lean towards; those monsters are going to be born one way or another. It doesn't necessarily mean this thing exists with a benevolent purpose. It could even indeed be what initiated the whole problem, but it's not in itself where 1904-1 "come from."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was intentional actually; that you become one of the "babies" only after you're "born" back out of it, though maybe that's too magic-like. A steady change is probably creepier and would sound better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My God. That's genius. Never going to look at one of these things the same way again. Well done to you, sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent combination of bizarre and creepy. The writing could use a bit of brushing up, especially in the first description paragraph, but I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Fetii" is correct, I believe, though most laypeople use "fetuses".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What on earth is wrong with those fetus? Also, what's plural for fetus?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
my only comment is, "GAH!"

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: See, the implication I got isn't that the playtubes are making the babies, but rather that the playset is somehow containing/focusing whatever phenomenon is actually doing it. Note how, when it was dismantled, the effect started spreading, but was immediately recontained once the playtubes were put back together.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
48 instances of SCP-1904-1 were subsequently discovered in the establishment's walk-in freezer

Is this … implying that … we've been eating them … ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeesh! Here's your +1…now make it go away!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. However, the playtubes = mentally retarded baby-making machine is kind of eh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh Jesus Christ how did you make this thing even creepier?

Anyway, yeah, you got my upvote. The containment procedures look much more professional now. :)

Only niggle I have is that "instantly regressing" implies that as soon as the D-class got out of the playground, but not before, he got brain damage. It seems like this would have happened instead inside the structure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't look at that picture any more, its very disturbing. Not necessarily a bad thing, just….Christ.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it just me, or is the second image box borked? I can't see any description text, and its top is almost under the first pic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Added a little to the containment procedures (I'm not good with these; even as a reader I usually find them unremarkable) but may need some point-for-point help with the tone and language here, and in fact all throughout. It feels totally off.

I changed the addendum to an incident report, and added another, not sure about it either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The containment features don't tell us what to do with SCP-1904-1 samples, or if we're supposed to clean the area around the darn thing, or, well, anything.

The description and Addendum are fine, but I'm downvoting because the containment procedure isn't properly described.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh geez. I remember the rough draft for this a while back. Interesting and appropriately disgusting, but I can't help the feeling that it's missing….something…..No vote for now.

Edit: Warmed up to it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The one time I entered one I stuck my hand into what I instantly knew was some other child's dried, sticky urine. I'm sure it contributed to the germophobia I had at an unusually young age.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was actually a little sad to see that go. It was at that point that I decided to +1. Perhaps give just minute hints as to what would occur, rather than filling out the full document? Either way, the +1 is yours.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hm, interesting and fresh. I like it.

And man those playhouses, I can't believe I liked them as a kid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was going somewhere with that, but changed my mind; gonna go ahead and take it out unless I actually do come up with an incident to write.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tone could definitely use some work, and the cop-out on why -1 instances aren't allowed back in is only marginally better than redaction, but not bad otherwise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I think of it like a "stopper" for some other force that can't be pinpointed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Being of an age where my contemporaries are being dull and having children the thought they could all suffer tragedy from an anomalous artefact guarded by the SCP is even more chilling. Great stuff!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Doesn't feel complete for some reason, but I like it anyways. +1

I'm a little rusty since my last (and still only) entry. I do think the tone and lingo still needs cleaning up, and I feel like it's missing something small, but significant; one of those twisted little details that might throw the reader for a loop. Or maybe it should be left simple?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Idea is fantastic, execution is good, hook is solid. Tone is a little iffy, but +1.
